search_queries_used: site:fascrs.org 2024 hemorrhoids guideline evaluation bleeding prolapse examination
site:dha.gov.ae hemorrhoids guideline UAE primary care
candidate_sources_rejected: rectal bleeding automatically attributed to haemorrhoids
intimate examination implied without consent and performance
automatic fibre, topical medicine, procedure, surgery, colonoscopy, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Other causes of bleeding, pain, mass, discharge, or bowel change require assessment.
No treatment or procedure is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults with haemorrhoidal symptoms; children, pregnancy, postpartum, inflammatory bowel disease, infection, immunocompromise, malignancy, anticoagulation, and severe active bleeding require tailored pathways.
setting_applicability: Primary-care documentation and follow-up as qualified by the exact source and clinical context.
UAE_applicability: International evidence requires UAE primary-care, laboratory, prescribing, referral, safeguarding, and local-pathway adaptation.
recency_verification: Exact official documents and sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The cited exact source remains current on the issuing organisation website at the review date.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact ASCRS evaluation section supports bowel habits, bleeding, prolapse, discomfort, thrombosis, hygiene, prior interventions, duration, impact, and clinician-performed focused examination without confirming haemorrhoids.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports disease-specific history, bowel habits, bleeding, prolapse, thrombosis, prior interventions, and clinician-performed focused examination.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact follow-up section supports documenting prior treatment, outcomes, complications, patient questions, clinician-entered discussion, and arranged follow-up without generating fibre, medicine, procedure, surgery, or referral advice.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documentation of clinician-reviewed prior treatment, outcomes, complications, and entered follow-up decisions without creating treatment advice.

unresolved_source_gaps: Bleeding amount, bowel pattern, pregnancy, consent, chaperone, examination, diagnosis, anaemia, tests, treatment, procedure, referral, urgency, and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.

All previously supported GP legacy-item mappings were conservatively removed because exact item identity, direct support, and item-specific applicability were not independently established under the fail-closed contract.
